content.problemStatement:
Chef is interested in the sum of GCDs of all prefixes of a permutation of the integers {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}.

Formally, for a permutation P=[P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN]P = [P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N]P=[P1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã] of {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}, let us define a function Fi=gcd‚Å°(A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶,Ai)F_i = \gcd(A_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_i)Fi‚Äã=gcd(A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai‚Äã). Chef is interested in the value of F1+F2+‚Ä¶+FNF_1 + F_2 + \ldots + F_NF1‚Äã+F2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+FN‚Äã.

Now, Chef wants to find a permutation of {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} which has the given sum equal to XXX. Please help Chef find one such permutation. In case there is no such permutation, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1. In case of multiple answers, any of them will be accepted.

A permutation of {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} is a sequence of numbers from 111 to NNN in which each number occurs exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing two space separated integers NNN and XXX denoting the length of required permutation and the required sum of GCDs of all prefixes respectively.


Output Format

If there is a valid permutation that satisfies the required condition, then:

In a single line, output NNN space-separated integers denoting the required permutation permutation.


If there is no permutation, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1 in a single line.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only possible permutation has a sum of 111, as required.
Test case 222: The two permutations of length 222 are:

[1,2][1, 2][1,2] with a value of 1+1=21 + 1 = 21+1=2
[2,1][2, 1][2,1] with a value of 2+1=32 + 1 = 32+1=3

None of them have a sum of X=1X = 1X=1, so we output ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Test case 333: For P=[2,4,3,1]P = [2, 4, 3, 1]P=[2,4,3,1], we have:

F1=2F_1 = 2F1‚Äã=2
F2=gcd‚Å°(2,4)=2F_2 = \gcd(2, 4) = 2F2‚Äã=gcd(2,4)=2
F3=gcd‚Å°(2,4,3)=1F_3 = \gcd(2, 4, 3) = 1F3‚Äã=gcd(2,4,3)=1
F4=gcd‚Å°(2,4,3,1)=1F_4 = \gcd(2, 4, 3, 1) = 1F4‚Äã=gcd(2,4,3,1)=1

The sum of these values is 666, as required.
